statement.focus_question: Let $1\leq a_1<\cdots <a_k\leq n$ be integers such that all sums of the shape $\sum_{u\leq i\leq v}a_i$ are distinct. Let $f(n)$ be the maximal such $k$. How does $f(n)$ grow?
statement.normalized: Let $1\leq a_1<\cdots <a_k\leq n$ be integers such that all sums of the shape $\sum_{u\leq i\leq v}a_i$ are distinct. Let $f(n)$ be the maximal such $k$. How does $f(n)$ grow? Is $f(n)=o(n)$?
statement.original:
Let $1\leq a_1<\cdots <a_k\leq n$ be integers such that all sums of the shape $\sum_{u\leq i\leq v}a_i$ are distinct. Let $f(n)$ be the maximal such $k$.

How does $f(n)$ grow? Is $f(n)=o(n)$?
